concerns.text: T015 explicitly caps the dataset at N=1,000 to meet Plan constraints, directly violating SC-003 which mandates N ≥ 5,000 for statistical power. The task implements a scope reduction without a corresponding amendment to the Success Criterion or a documented statistical power analysis justifying why N=1,000 is sufficient. This is a silent weakening of the spec's acceptance criteria.

T025b hardcodes the threshold as '70% of mean accuracy'. FR-005 and the Spec explicitly define the threshold as '[deferred] of the dataset's mean accuracy' to allow dynamic determination. Hardcoding 70% violates the constitutional principle of deferring empirical values and contradicts the spec's requirement for a dynamic baseline, effectively weakening the FR-005 constraint.

T011 instructs the script to fallback to 'Reconstruction-Only' mode if independent annotations are missing. FR-002 explicitly mandates execution of three distinct tasks (summarization, bug detection, function completion) requiring independent annotations for the first two. Allowing a fallback to a single task type without an explicit scope reduction in the spec constitutes a silent relaxation of the functional requirement.

T027 describes applying multiple-comparison error correction but lacks a verification step to measure or report the resulting error rate. SC-005 requires the system to 'measure' the error rate against the correction method. The task delivers the mechanism but fails to deliver the verification metric required by the Success Criterion.
